metadata.name: Docker Healthcheck Audit
metadata.description: Audits Docker containers for missing healthchecks and optionally fails the playbook when violations are found. Can save a JSON audit report to the target host.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This playbook audits containers for missing Docker healthchecks.

## Prerequisites
- Decide whether the playbook should fail when missing healthchecks are found.
- Confirm the report path is writable if you plan to save the audit output.

## Run
1. Render `main.yml` and review the failure and report options.
2. Run the playbook against the target Docker hosts.
3. Review the output and update the affected container definitions as needed.

## Docs
- [Docker HEALTHCHECK](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck)

items.name: audit_fail_on_missing
items.title: Fail Missing Checks
items.description: Fail the play if one or more containers are missing a healthcheck

items.title: Save JSON Report
items.description: Save audit report to a JSON file on the target host
